acceptance_targets: [chapter_13_craft_001] Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:633 shows one complete work beat from symptom or fragment through argument to recorded decision, with a concrete line entered into the registry or a concrete care outcome. Fail if those passages still summarize the labor as a list of cases or generic routine.

[chapter_13_prose_001] Pass if at least six of the cited `as if` lines are rewritten into non-`as if` constructions and the revised passages still communicate the same image through direct detail or action. Fail if the same comparison frame still dominates the cited beats.

[chapter_13_prose_002] Pass if the revised sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:299-335 contains at least three distinct Mira interior beats and no longer runs 10 or more consecutive paragraphs on external action and dialogue alone. Fail if Mira's interior lens still disappears until the grief-reading at line 337.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:633 shows one complete work beat from symptom or fragment through argument to recorded decision, with a concrete line entered into the registry or a concrete care outcome. Fail if those passages still summarize the labor as a list of cases or generic routine.
findings.problem: The chapter announces that Blue Hour has become real desk work and that the registry is becoming method, but at both pivots it drops into montage summary. That compression blunts the tactical burden of triage and documentation right where the chapter needs lived process, not recap.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:633, replace at least one summary block with a full on-page case or registry dispute. Put a named client or named trace in the chair or on the page, force one disagreement about what counts as evidence, and end on a specific entry, refusal, or triage consequence rather than omnibus summary.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least six of the cited `as if` lines are rewritten into non-`as if` constructions and the revised passages still communicate the same image through direct detail or action. Fail if the same comparison frame still dominates the cited beats.
findings.problem: The prose leans on the same `as if` comparison chassis so often that distinct observations start arriving in one mechanical sentence shape. By the back half, the repetition reads as autopilot rather than style.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the comparison-heavy beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:179, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:315, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:413, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:635 first. Replace most of those `as if` constructions with direct bodily description, action, or sharper one-off metaphors so the image changes shape instead of repeating the same hinge.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:299-335 contains at least three distinct Mira interior beats and no longer runs 10 or more consecutive paragraphs on external action and dialogue alone. Fail if Mira's interior lens still disappears until the grief-reading at line 337.
findings.problem: The Pelican Courts approach and threshold exchange run for more than a dozen short paragraphs with almost no Mira-specific thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment. That stretches the close-third contract thin and makes the scene read more like blocking than lived perception until line 337 restores her interpretive lens.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread Mira back into snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:299, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:303, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:315, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:329 with brief interior beats tied to the stairs, Gabe's strain, the risk of showing the notebook, or the hearing's aftertaste. Keep the additions local and bodily so the scene stays moving while regaining interior presence.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into biotech grime, erotic awkwardness, debt language, bureaucratic absurdity, and the tenderness of people maintaining one another under bad systems. When choosing between an elegant abstraction and an ugly specific detail, choose the specific detail unless omission itself heightens pressure.
aesthetic_risk_policy.edge_case_resolution: Use restraint only when aftermath, gesture, residue, or changed behavior keeps the full pressure visible. Do not convert morally compromising acts into self-exonerating reflection, and do not flatten exploitative sex, poverty, or violence into generic dystopian mood.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches injury, sex, class shame, or institutional cruelty and the language retreats into tasteful fog, rewrite until the bodily fact, immediate sensation, and social consequence are fully legible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the norm in spoken English here; uncontracted forms are reserved for legal testimony, managerial performance, deliberate emphasis, or characters whose formality is itself a tactic.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate inspection-room procedural chapters with crowded comic clinic scenes, quiet domestic intervals, erotic pressure, warehouse horror, and civic hearing sequences; consecutive chapters should differ in scene density, dialogue load, and emotional temperature so the book keeps changing how it moves.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, urban, bodily, and unsentimental; technical vocabulary is welcome when embedded in action and cost, not as decorative futurism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third-person close anchored to Mira Alves; the narration can infer social systems through what she notices, but it should not become omniscient exposition.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate crisp claim-note precision with longer, salt-wet, bodily sentences when feeling, memory, or city pressure breaches Mira's control.
